scope: S4096 layer-2 source-boundary sparse-MLA score/prob/scaled-dP producer checksum on one MI350
read: Widening the selected window matters. Moving from selected64 to selected512 removes repeated row-term work and reduces CTA count, cutting the source-boundary producer checksum from 11.51 ms to 4.79 ms. This is the first lower-level source-boundary producer surface below the current 11.13 ms full sparse-attention backward boundary, but it is not promoted: it emits only a checksum, not dQ or valid dKV, and it is still 1.51x over the 3.16 ms producer budget implied by the materialized HSK dQ+dKV floor.

why: The selected512 producer-only latency is useful and much better than selected64, but dQ and dKV writeback would add work on top of 4.79 ms.
The strict source-boundary budget is 3.16 ms before a full dQ+dKV implementation can beat the current 11.13 ms boundary with the 7.97 ms materialized floor.
This one-token CTA schedule has no route-local write reduction: route_atomic_reduction_x remains 1.0 even at selected512.
next_kernel_contract: Add token tiling or persistent producer-consumer ownership so a CTA/wave group handles more than one token without recomputing selected KV work wastefully.
Move from checksum to a tiny score page consumed by D-parallel dQ/dKV consumers only after producer-only is at or below 3.16 ms.
Do not run 4x/8x TFLOPs materialization for this path until full source-boundary dQ+dKV beats 11.13 ms.
